mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of cache coherence protocols?

options.text: To ensure consistency of data in multiple caches.
To increase cache memory size.
To reduce main memory access time.
To improve single processor performance.

mcqs.explanation: Cache coherence protocols ensure that all processors in a multiprocessor system have a consistent view of shared memory by managing how cached copies are updated and invalidated.

mcqs.question: In the MESI protocol, which state indicates that a cache line is modified and different from main memory?

options.text: Shared
Modified
Exclusive
Invalid

mcqs.explanation: The Modified (M) state indicates the cache line has been written to and differs from main memory. This cache holds the only valid copy and must write it back to memory before another cache can read it.

mcqs.question: What is the main advantage of directory-based coherence protocols over snooping protocols?

options.text: Lower hardware complexity.
Better scalability for large processor counts.
Faster response time for all operations.
Simpler implementation.

mcqs.explanation: Directory-based protocols scale better because they avoid broadcast traffic. Instead of snooping all bus transactions, they send coherence messages only to caches that actually hold copies of the data.

mcqs.question: Which phenomenon occurs when processors access different variables that happen to reside on the same cache line?

options.text: True sharing
Cache pollution
False sharing
Coherence thrashing

mcqs.explanation: False sharing occurs when unrelated variables on the same cache line cause unnecessary invalidations. The processors are not truly sharing data, but the cache line granularity of the coherence protocol creates the appearance of sharing.

mcqs.question: In a write-invalidate protocol, what happens when a processor writes to a shared memory location?

options.text: All other cached copies are invalidated.
All other cached copies are updated with the new value.
The write is delayed until all caches acknowledge.
The main memory is updated but caches are unchanged.

mcqs.explanation: In write-invalidate protocols, a write operation causes all other cached copies of that memory location to be marked as Invalid. Subsequent reads by other processors will cause a cache miss and fetch the updated value.

mcqs.question: What advantage does the Exclusive state in MESI provide over the simpler MSI protocol?

options.text: It allows multiple caches to write simultaneously.
It allows a silent transition to Modified on write without bus traffic.
It eliminates the need for the Invalid state.
It reduces the total number of cache lines needed.

mcqs.explanation: In MSI, a write to a clean-only-copy requires a bus transaction (Shared -> Modified). In MESI, the Exclusive state tells the cache it has the sole copy, so it can write (Exclusive -> Modified) without generating any bus traffic, reducing overhead.

mcqs.question: How can false sharing be mitigated in a parallel program?

options.text: By using more processors.
By padding data so that per-thread variables are on separate cache lines.
By switching from write-invalidate to write-update protocol.
By increasing the cache line size.

mcqs.explanation: Padding data structures ensures that variables frequently written by different threads are placed on separate cache lines. This eliminates the unnecessary invalidations caused by unrelated data sharing a cache line.
